rationale: Gate's json:"gate,omitempty" tag matches R6/R9's pin exactly (name/type/omitempty/parse-lenient are the stable contract). Empirically verified (scratch /tmp probe): an absent gate key and an explicit gate:"" both unmarshal to Gate="" and re-marshal to byte-identical output (key omitted) -- deterministic, and identical to the existing AbandonReason/ApproveThresholdExpr omitempty precedent already in Panel, so this isn't a novel semantic, it's consistent hygiene. TestPanel_GateFieldDecisionInert's legacy sub-test confirms a gate-less panel.json parses field-for-field identical to pre-bead behavior, and an unexpected-but-valid string ("weird") round-trips without setting Registration.Err, matching R6's parse-lenient clause. A genuine JSON type violation (gate:123) fails the whole Unmarshal and sets Registration.Err at the per-directory Scan level -- identical to how every other typed Panel field already behaves on a type mismatch, not a new gap (and squarely F1's adversarial lens, not a defect here). Struct-tag style, placement (appended after ApproveThresholdExpr, following historical append order), and doc-comment depth mirror the existing fields. Confirmed no internal/config import (leaf invariant holds). Full internal/panel suite, go vet, gofmt all clean.

findings.description: The struct-level JSON-schema doc comment atop Panel (panel.go lines 36-42) still lists only the original fields plus abandoned/abandon_reason; it was never updated for approve_threshold (109) and isn't updated for gate here either. Pre-existing drift, not introduced by this bead, and each field's own inline comment is authoritative and thorough -- non-blocking.
